 {"id":"bd-3954","title":"Task: Create FileReservationWatcher module","description":"## Purpose
Create a background watcher that monitors pane output and auto-reserves files.

## Module Location
internal/watcher/file_reservation.go

## FileReservationWatcher Struct
type FileReservationWatcher struct {
    client             *agentmail.Client
    projectDir         string
    activeReservations map[string]time.Time  // pane -> last activity
    mu                 sync.Mutex
}

## Key Methods
func (w *FileReservationWatcher) Start(ctx context.Context)
func (w *FileReservationWatcher) checkPaneOutputs()
func (w *FileReservationWatcher) OnFileEdit(pane Pane, files []string)
func (w *FileReservationWatcher) releaseIdleReservations()

## Watcher Loop
- Poll every 10 seconds
- For each pane, capture recent output
- Parse for file edit patterns
- Reserve new files detected
- Release reservations for idle panes (10m+ inactive)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Background watcher starts with session
- Detects file edits from pane output
- Reserves files automatically in Agent Mail
- Releases reservations on idle
- Thread-safe operation

## Technical Notes
Use goroutine with context for lifecycle
Coordinate with session lifecycle events","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:41:19.522299399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T16:11:37.901182127Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T16:11:37.901094762Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3954","depends_on_id":"bd-dz3r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:58:25Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-396n1","title":"process_triage: Add dashboard health indicators per agent","description":"Add health status indicators to agent entries in dashboard.

## Design

### Indicator Placement
Next to each agent/pane name in the dashboard.

### Visual States
- 🟢 useful/waiting: Agent is working or legitimately waiting
- 🟡 idle (>5 min): Agent finished, no new work
- 🔴 stuck (>10 min): Agent appears unresponsive
- ⚫ zombie: Process not running properly

### Enhanced Pane List
```
┌─ Agents ───────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 🟢 agent-1   claude-code    Working (5m)       │
│ 🟡 agent-2   codex          Idle (8m)          │
│ 🔴 agent-3   gemini         Stuck (15m) ⚠️     │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Alert Behavior
When agent is stuck:
- Indicator turns red
- Optional alert badge (⚠️)
- Click to see details/options

## Implementation

```go
func (m *DashboardModel) renderAgentStatus(pane string) string {
    state := m.healthMonitor.GetState(pane)
    
    switch state.Classification {
    case "useful", "waiting":
        return lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("green")).Render("🟢")
    case "idle":
        return lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("yellow")).Render("🟡")
    case "stuck":
        return lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("red")).Render("🔴")
    default:
        return "⚫"
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-398bh","title":"RCH: Implement PreToolUse hook configuration for build interception","description":"Configure PreToolUse hook to intercept and route builds through RCH.

## Hook Configuration

For Claude Code agents, configure hook at spawn time:
```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PreToolUse": [{
      "matcher": "Bash",
      "command": "rch intercept --command \"\""
    }]
  }
}
```

## RCH Intercept Logic

The rch intercept command:
1. Parses command to identify build tool
2. Checks if command matches intercept patterns
3. If match and workers available, return replacement command
4. If no match or no workers, return original command

## Implementation

```go
func ConfigureRCHHook(agentConfig *AgentConfig) error {
    if !config.Integrations.RCH.Enabled {
        return nil
    }
    
    status := rch.GetStatus()
    if !status.Available || status.HealthyCount == 0 {
        // No RCH or no workers, skip hook
        return nil
    }
    
    // Add hook to agent config
    agentConfig.Hooks.PreToolUse = append(agentConfig.Hooks.PreToolUse, Hook{
        Matcher: "Bash",
        Command: "rch intercept --command \"\"",
    })
    
    return nil
}
```

 {"id":"bd-39uo","title":"Task: Implement interactive Bubble Tea wizard","description":"## Purpose
Create an interactive setup wizard for non-technical users using Bubble Tea TUI framework.

## Wizard Flow
1. Project type selection (Go/Python/Node/Rust/Other)
2. Agent count configuration
3. Integration toggles (Agent Mail, CASS, CM)
4. File reservation auto-enable toggle
5. Confirmation and summary

## UI Components
- Selection list for project type
- Number input for agent count
- Toggle/checkbox for integrations
- Progress indicator during setup
- Summary display at end

## Acceptance Criteria
- Wizard runs by default, --non-interactive skips it
- All options configurable through wizard
- Escape key exits cleanly
- Summary shows all chosen options before confirming

## Technical Notes
Use existing Bubble Tea patterns from internal/tui/
Match dashboard styling (Catppuccin theme)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:31:55.603636143Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:07:55.578235604Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:07:55.578151726Z","close_reason":"Interactive Bubble Tea wizard already implemented in init_wizard.go","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39uo","depends_on_id":"bd-38gl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:58:25Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3acv","title":"Task: Create Archiver background service","description":"## Purpose
Implement background archiver that captures agent output for CASS indexing.

## Archiver Struct
type Archiver struct {
    sessionName string
    outputDir   string
    interval    time.Duration
    lastOffsets map[string]int // pane -> last archived line
}

## Archive Loop
func (a *Archiver) Run(ctx context.Context) {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(a.interval)
    for {
        select {
        case <-ctx.Done():
            a.flush() // Final archive on shutdown
            return
        case <-ticker.C:
            a.archiveNewContent()
        }
    }
}

## Output Format (CASS-compatible JSONL)
{"session": "myproject", "pane": "cc_1", "agent": "claude", "timestamp": "2026-01-19T10:30:00Z", "content": "..."}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Background archiving with configurable interval
- Incremental capture (no duplicates)
- Flush on shutdown
- CASS-compatible output format

## Technical Notes
Use goroutine with context
Store archive in .ntm/archive/","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-p3en","title":"Task: Define command tier taxonomy and categorization","description":"## Overview
Create the foundational tier taxonomy that categorizes all NTM commands into proficiency levels.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/cli/tiers/taxonomy.go defining:
   - Tier enum (Apprentice=1, Journeyman=2, Master=3)
   - CommandTier struct with {Command, MinTier, Description, Examples}
   - Global registry of all commands with their tier assignments

2. Tier 1 (Apprentice) - Essential workflow:
   - spawn: Create agent sessions
   - send: Send prompts to agents
   - status: View running sessions
   - kill: Stop sessions
   - help: Get help (tier-aware)

3. Tier 2 (Journeyman) - Full standard:
   - All Tier 1 commands
   - dashboard: Interactive TUI
   - context: View context window usage
   - tail: View agent output
   - interrupt: Send Ctrl+C to agents
   - swarm: Swarm orchestration
   - All standard flags

4. Tier 3 (Master) - Advanced/experimental:
   - All Tier 2 commands
   - --robot-* flags for automation
   - Internal debugging commands
   - Experimental features

## Implementation Notes
- Use Go constants for tier values
- Include help text per tier explaining what unlocks next
- Document promotion criteria in tier definitions

## Acceptance Criteria
- taxonomy.go compiles and exports tier constants
- All existing commands have tier assignments
- Tier progression is logical (each tier builds on previous)
- Unit tests verify taxonomy completeness","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:24:07.875431526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:03:20.700671521Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:03:20.700604966Z","close_reason":"Implemented command tier taxonomy in internal/cli/tiers/taxonomy.go with Tier enum, CommandInfo struct, and complete Registry mapping all commands","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-p3en","depends_on_id":"bd-7ny3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:24:07.905286067Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-pjjk7","title":"EPIC: Canonical Assignment Flow (ntm assign + spawn --assign)","description":"## Goal
Reduce user confusion by providing **one** canonical assignment flow and **one** convenience entry point.

## Decision
- **Keep**: `ntm assign` as the only assignment command.
- **Keep**: `ntm spawn --assign` as the only convenience entry (spawn + assign).
- **Expose**: `--strategy=balanced|speed|quality|dependency|round-robin` on `ntm assign`.
- **Drop/Merge**: `ntm swarm`, `ntm send --distribute`, YAML swarm config, and from‑file assignments.

## Why
Multiple entry points doing the same thing (assign work to agents) multiplies docs, tests, and code paths and will confuse users. The project benefits from a single, well‑designed path.

## Scope
- Keep existing strategies (`balanced`, `speed`, `quality`, `dependency`) intact and documented.
- Add `round‑robin` as an **optional** additional strategy (no renaming of existing ones).
- Implement assignment tracking + reporting so `balanced` can be truly load‑aware.
- Implement `ntm spawn --assign` as a thin wrapper over `ntm assign`.
- Update docs/help to emphasize canonical flow.

## Non‑Goals
- No new orchestration commands outside `assign` + `spawn --assign`.
- No YAML swarm config or additional CLI entry points.

## Success Criteria
- `ntm assign` is the only path for distributing work.
- `ntm spawn --assign` works end‑to‑end with clear output.
- Redundant commands are closed/dropped.
- Strategy list in docs/help matches code.","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:57:08.450596296Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T21:45:24.089748119Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pjjk7","depends_on_id":"bd-22nh8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:57:08.478922178Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-pqwk","title":"Support partial/prefix session names for disambiguation","description":"Currently you need to type the full session name like `vnt destructive_command_guard`. Should support prefix matching like `vnt destr` as long as it uniquely matches one session.

**Behavior:**
- If prefix matches exactly one session: use it
- If prefix matches multiple sessions: show matching options and ask user to be more specific
- If prefix matches zero sessions: show error with available sessions

**Example:**
```bash
# Currently required
ntm view destructive_command_guard

# Desired
ntm view destr  # works if only one session starts with 'destr'
vnt destr       # same with alias
```

This would make the CLI much more ergonomic for daily use.","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

-{"id":"bd-ptu2","title":"Task: Add event markers and details overlay","description":"## Overview
Add discrete event markers on the timeline with hover/select details.

## Deliverables
1. Event marker types:
   - Prompt sent (▶): When user sends prompt to agent
   - Completion (✓): When agent finishes a task
   - Error (✗): When error detected
   - Start/Stop (◆): Session lifecycle events

2. Marker rendering:
   - Small icons above timeline bar at event time
   - Different colors for different event types
   - Clustered markers when events too close together

3. Details overlay:
   - Show popup when marker selected
   - Include: timestamp, event type, details
   - For prompts: first 50 chars of message
   - For errors: error message

4. Interaction:
   - Arrow keys to navigate between markers
   - Enter to expand marker details
   - Esc to close overlay

## Implementation Notes
- Use unicode symbols for markers
- Popup positioning to avoid edge overflow
- Cache marker positions for efficient navigation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Markers appear at correct times
- Different event types visually distinct
- Details overlay shows on selection
- Navigation between markers works
- Unit tests for marker placement","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:03.496917374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:50.481943421Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ptu2","depends_on_id":"bd-3h0n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:50.481921380Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ptu2","depends_on_id":"bd-f815","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:03.512975659Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-qjsu","title":"E2E Tests: File reservation with real Agent Mail","description":"## E2E Test: ntm + Agent Mail File Reservation Integration

**Note:** File reservation is implemented in MCP Agent Mail. This tests ntm's integration with that existing system.

### Prerequisites
- MCP Agent Mail server running (`am` health check passes)
- Test workspace with beads

### Test Scenarios

1. **Basic reservation flow**
   - Spawn agent via ntm
   - Assign bead with file paths
   - Verify Agent Mail shows reservation for agent
   - Complete bead
   - Verify reservation released

2. **Conflict detection**
   - Spawn 2 agents
   - Agent 1 assigned bead touching foo.go
   - Agent 2 assigned bead touching foo.go
   - Verify ntm reports conflict from Agent Mail
   - Verify holder info shown

3. **Reservation on assign**
   - Run ntm assign with --auto
   - Verify file_reservation_paths called for each assignment
   - Check Agent Mail state reflects reservations

### Verification Commands
```bash
# Check Agent Mail for reservations
# (use Agent Mail MCP tools or am CLI)
```

### Logging
- Log Agent Mail API calls
- Log reservation state at each step
- Log conflict details

## Test File
tests/e2e/test_file_reservation_integration.sh","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:14:22.491105889Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T02:46:46.058716925Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T02:46:46.058672331Z","close_reason":"Added e2e Agent Mail reservation tests; go test blocked by no space left on device","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qjsu","depends_on_id":"bd-dz3r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:14:22.511980074Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-qjsu","depends_on_id":"bd-r5vz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:19:51.099332584Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- Mock filesystem watcher
- Fixture files for various scenarios

 {"id":"bd-r6d5e","title":"E2E Tests: ntm spawn --assign full workflow","description":"## Test Coverage
Full end-to-end test of ntm spawn --assign convenience flag.

## Test Scenario
1. Create test workspace with beads
2. Run ntm spawn --assign (single command)
3. Verify agents spawned
4. Verify beads assigned
5. Verify prompts received by agents
6. Verify combined output

## Test Script (tests/e2e/test_spawn_assign.sh)
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

LOG=/tmp/ntm-e2e-spawn-assign-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log
exec > >(tee -a $LOG) 2>&1

echo "[INFO] Starting ntm spawn --assign E2E test at $(date)"

# Setup
echo "[STEP 1] Creating test workspace..."
TEST_DIR=/tmp/ntm-spawn-assign-test-$$
mkdir -p $TEST_DIR
cd $TEST_DIR
br init
for i in {1..4}; do br create "Test bead $i" --type task; done
echo "[OK] Created 4 test beads"

# Spawn + Assign in one command
echo "[STEP 2] Running ntm spawn --assign..."
RESULT=$(ntm spawn ntm-spawn-assign-$$ --cc=2 --no-user --assign --strategy=round-robin --json)
echo "[DEBUG] Result: $RESULT"

# Verify spawn
echo "[STEP 3] Verifying spawn..."
AGENT_COUNT=$(echo $RESULT | jq '.spawn.agents | length')
if [ "$AGENT_COUNT" -ne 2 ]; then
    echo "[FAIL] Expected 2 agents, got $AGENT_COUNT"
    exit 1
fi
echo "[OK] 2 agents spawned"

# Verify assign
echo "[STEP 4] Verifying assignments..."
ASSIGNED=$(echo $RESULT | jq '.assign.assigned | length')
if [ "$ASSIGNED" -lt 2 ]; then
    echo "[FAIL] Expected at least 2 assignments, got $ASSIGNED"
    exit 1
fi
echo "[OK] $ASSIGNED beads assigned"

# Cleanup
echo "[STEP 5] Cleaning up..."
ntm kill ntm-spawn-assign-$$
rm -rf $TEST_DIR
echo "[OK] Cleanup complete"

echo "[PASS] ntm spawn --assign E2E test passed at $(date)"
```

## Logging Requirements
- Timestamp each step
- Log combined output
- Log spawn and assign phases separately
- Save full log to /tmp

-{"id":"ntm-l3hh","title":"Add user confirmation option for rotation","description":"## Overview
Allow users to require confirmation before auto-rotation.

## Location
Extend `internal/context/rotation.go`

## Configuration
```toml
[context_rotation]
require_confirmation = false  # Default: auto-rotate without asking
confirmation_timeout_sec = 60 # How long to wait for response
```

## Confirmation Flow
When rotation is triggered and confirmation required:

1. **Pause new work** to the agent
2. **Show notification** in dashboard:
   ```
   ┌─ Rotation Required ────────────────────────┐
   │ Agent cc_1 (architect) is at 85% context   │
   │                                            │
   │ Options:                                   │
   │   [R] Rotate now                           │
   │   [C] Try compact first                    │
   │   [I] Ignore (continue as-is)              │
   │   [P] Postpone 30 minutes                  │
   │                                            │
   │ Auto-rotate in: 45s                        │
   └────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   ```
3. **Wait for response** or timeout
4. **Execute choice** or default action

## Robot Mode Output
```json
{
  "type": "rotation_pending",
  "agent_id": "cc_1",
  "context_percent": 85.2,
  "awaiting_confirmation": true,
  "timeout_seconds": 45,
  "options": ["rotate", "compact", "ignore", "postpone"]
}
```

## CLI Override
```bash
ntm rotation confirm cc_1 --action=rotate
ntm rotation confirm cc_1 --action=compact
ntm rotation confirm cc_1 --action=ignore
ntm rotation confirm cc_1 --action=postpone --minutes=30
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Configuration option
- [ ] Dashboard notification UI
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts for options
- [ ] Timeout with default action
- [ ] Robot mode pending notification
- [ ] CLI confirmation command
- [ ] Tests for confirmation flow","notes":"## Progress Update (2026-01-21)
- Completed message handling wiring: Added refreshPendingRotations constant, fetchPendingRotations function, PendingRotationsUpdateMsg handler
- Completed data fetching: Added to Init() and fullRefresh() cycles
- Completed keybinding handlers: Added RotationConfirmActionMsg type and handling for r/c/i/p keys in rotation_confirm.go
- Added executeRotationConfirmAction() function to handle confirmation actions (rotate, compact, ignore, postpone)
- Added RotationConfirmResultMsg and handler for action results with healthMessage feedback
- All rotation confirm panel tests pass
- Build succeeds

Remaining items (from original task):
- [ ] CLI confirmation command (ntm rotation confirm)
- [ ] Integration testing with actual pending rotations

 {"id":"ntm-l4fp","title":"Add Agent Mail notifications for scan results","description":"## Overview
Integrate UBS scan results with Agent Mail to notify coordinating agents about code quality findings, enabling proactive collaboration on fixes.

## Background
When multiple AI agents work on a codebase, scan results are valuable coordination signals:
- New critical findings need immediate attention
- Findings in files an agent is editing should be surfaced
- Scan summaries help agents prioritize work

## Implementation Details

### Notification Types
1. **Scan Summary** - After each scan, send digest to all active agents
   - Total findings by severity
   - New vs. existing findings
   - Files with most issues

2. **Targeted Alerts** - Notify specific agents about relevant findings
   - Match findings to file reservations
   - Agent editing auth.go gets auth-related findings
   - Importance: high for critical, normal for others

3. **Fix Confirmations** - When findings are resolved
   - Notify original reporter
   - Update thread if findings were discussed

### Message Templates
```markdown
Subject: [Scan] {n} new findings in {project}

## Scan Summary
- Critical: {critical_count}
- Errors: {error_count}  
- Warnings: {warning_count}

## New Findings
{finding_list}

## Your Files
The following findings affect files you're working on:
{agent_relevant_findings}
```

### Files to Create/Modify
- internal/scanner/notify.go - Notification logic
- internal/scanner/matcher.go - File reservation matching
- internal/cli/scan.go - Add --notify flag

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scan summaries sent to registered agents
- [ ] Findings matched to file reservations
- [ ] Appropriate importance levels set
- [ ] Notifications are concise and actionable","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T08:33:31.765440Z","updated_at":"2025-12-10T21:56:58.898130Z","closed_at":"2025-12-10T21:56:58.898130Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-l4fp","depends_on_id":"ntm-lk66","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:58:22Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"ntm-l4fp","depends_on_id":"ntm-p77j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:58:22Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-l5h","title":"EPIC: Comprehensive Testing Infrastructure","description":"# EPIC: Comprehensive Testing Infrastructure

## Background & Rationale

NTM (Named Tmux Manager) currently has very low test coverage across most packages. This EPIC addresses the need for:

1. **Full Unit Test Coverage** - Testing each function/method in isolation WITHOUT mocks or fakes where possible, using real dependencies
2. **Integration Tests** - Testing component interactions (CLI → tmux, config → commands, etc.)
3. **End-to-End (E2E) Tests** - Full workflow tests with detailed logging for debugging

## Current Coverage Analysis

| Package | Current Coverage | Status |
|---------|-----------------|--------|
| cmd/ntm | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/cli | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/config | 16.2% | 🟡 Needs improvement |
| internal/output | 55.1% | 🟡 Good start |
| internal/palette | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/robot | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/status | 69.6% | 🟢 Good (has failures) |
| internal/tmux | 5.4% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/tui/* | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/tutorial | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/updater | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/watcher | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |

## Testing Philosophy

### No Mocks/Fakes Policy
- Use REAL tmux sessions for tmux tests (create/destroy per test)
- Use REAL file system for config tests
- Use REAL HTTP calls where applicable
- Only mock external services that are impractical (GitHub API rate limits, etc.)

### Detailed Logging Strategy
- All E2E tests output structured logs with timestamps
- Log file per test run with full command output
- Capture stderr/stdout separately
- Include system state before/after each test

## Success Criteria
- 80%+ coverage on all non-TUI packages
- 60%+ coverage on TUI packages (harder to test without mocks)
- All E2E tests pass on macOS and Linux
- Tests complete in <5 minutes total
- No flaky tests (3 consecutive passes required)

## Technical Considerations
- Tests requiring tmux must be skipped in CI without tmux
- Use Go's testing.Short() for quick vs full test modes
- Parallelize where safe (different tmux sessions)
- Clean up resources even on test failure","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T08:17:38.345103Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T03:38:02.593734Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T03:38:02.593734Z","close_reason":"All children completed: Unit Test Coverage (ntm-idx), Integration Tests (ntm-7lk), E2E Tests (ntm-vr2), spawn tests (ntm-gnyw). Comprehensive testing infrastructure in place.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ntm-l5y","title":"Integrate beads into robot snapshot","description":"Include beads project status in unified snapshot.

## Rationale
Beads is how we track work. The snapshot needs to include bead summary so orchestrator knows what work is available, blocked, and in-progress.

## Integration Points
1. Detect if .beads/ directory exists
2. Run bd stats or equivalent query
3. Include summary counts and optionally ready list

## Snapshot Extension
```json
{
  "...": "existing fields",
  "beads": {
    "project": "/path/to/project",
    "summary": {
      "total": 58,
      "open": 45,
      "in_progress": 3,
      "blocked": 15,
      "ready": 27,
      "closed": 13
    },
    "ready_preview": [
      {"id": "ntm-1rz", "title": "Create profiler package", "priority": "P0"},
      {"id": "ntm-7mg", "title": "Add design tokens", "priority": "P1"}
    ],
    "in_progress": [
      {"id": "ntm-abc", "title": "Implement feature X", "assigned": "BlueLake"}
    ]
  }
}
```

## Ready Preview Limit
- Default 5 items
- --robot-snapshot --bead-limit=10 to change
- Sorted by priority then age

## Graceful Degradation
```json
{"beads": {"available": false, "reason": "no .beads/ directory"}}
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenCastle","created_at":"2025-12-10T07:53:26.959482Z","updated_at":"2025-12-10T08:58:54.711742Z","closed_at":"2025-12-10T08:58:54.711742Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
